description_long: This bird has a robust body with predominantly pale gray plumage covering its wings, back, and tail. Its head and upper chest are a soft pink, blending into the gray of the rest of the body, a distinctive feature of the Galah cockatoo. The beak is short, pale, and slightly curved, suited for cracking seeds. The wings are long and streamlined, with subtle feather layering visible along the sides. The tail is moderately long and appears slightly rounded at the tip. The legs are not clearly visible, but the overall form suggests a medium-sized parrot adapted for perching and ground foraging.
description: A medium-sized bird with pale gray wings and tail, and a pink head and chest, resembling a Galah cockatoo. It has a short, pale, curved beak and a streamlined body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bird, highlighting the pink head and chest blending into the gray wings and tail.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the rounded back and tail, with the wings folded closely along the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the pink and gray coloration, with the beak and head profile visible.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the pink face and chest, with the beak and head shape clearly visible.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid gray wings and tail with a pink head and chest, subtle gradient between colors
